row1.revenue: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a revenue of 716.9B USD, narrowly surpassing Walmart Inc. (WMT) by 3.7B USD. The top two companies hold a significant revenue lead over the third-ranked UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (UNH) at 269.3B USD.
row1.net_income: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a Net Income of 132.2B USD, outpacing NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) by 12.1B USD. The top 3 companies (GOOGL, NVDA, AAPL) account for 364.3B USD in combined Net Income, more than 4.7 times that of Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN).
row1.total_assets: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $4.4T in Total Assets, a significant gap of over $1T from Bank of America Corporation (BAC) in second place. This substantial gap underscores JPM's dominance in the industry.
row1.total_equity: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with $415.3B in Total Equity, outpacing Amazon.com (AMZN) by $4.2B. The top 3 companies hold a significant 54% of the total equity, indicating a concentrated market.
row1.total_liabilities: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads with Total Liabilities of 4.1T USD, nearly 7 times that of Bank of America Corporation (BAC) at 3.1T USD. This significant gap underscores the substantial scale of JPM's balance sheet.
row1.cash_and_equivalents: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) holds the top spot for Cash And Equivalents with $343.3B USD, a significant lead over Bank of America Corporation (BAC) at $239.3B USD, indicating a $104B USD gap between the two leaders.
row1.gross_profit: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a Gross Profit of $360.5B USD, nearly 50% more than the second-place Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) at $240.3B USD. This significant gap underscores AMZN's dominant position in the market.
row1.operating_income_ebit: AAPL (Apple Inc.) leads the pack with $133.1B in Operating Income Ebit, a significant gap of $12.7B to NVDA (NVIDIA Corporation) in second place. The top 3 companies (AAPL, NVDA, and GOOGL) collectively account for 55% of the total Operating Income Ebit among the listed companies.

row2_sector.revenue: Consumer Cyclical and Technology sectors dominate revenue generation with 43.1% combined market share, while Consumer Defensive and Communication Services round out the top four sectors. The top two sectors account for nearly a third of total revenue.
row2_sector.total_assets: Financial Services sector dominates the total assets landscape with 64.1% share, dwarfing other sectors. This concentration indicates a high reliance on the sector's financial health.
row2_sector.net_income: Technology sector dominates the net income landscape, accounting for 39.2% of the total with $341.0B USD, a significant lead over the next largest sector. The top two sectors (Technology and Communication Services) combined for 64% of total net income.

row2_bubble: NVDA's 55.6% net margin far surpasses UNH's 2.7%, indicating significant differences in profitability efficiency among companies, with NVDA's strong margins driven by its substantial revenue of 215.9B USD.
row3_heatmap: Morgan Stanley (MA) stands out for its exceptional return on equity (ROE) at 193.5%, indicating strong profitability. In contrast, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) takes on significant leverage with a debt-to-equity ratio of 11.21x, highlighting a risk-reward tradeoff.
row4_market_trends: Alphabet's (GOOGL) 12-month cumulative return of +104.4% stands out as the highest among the listed companies, while Netflix (NFLX) suffered the largest decline at -41.5%. This significant performance gap suggests a stark contrast in investor sentiment towards growth and value stocks. GOOGL's impressive return may be attributed to its diversified business portfolio and dominant market position.
